message.content:
You are given the following task to perform in Dafny:

Write a method that takes the lengths of the sides of a rectangle. Then return the area and perimeter of the rectangle and the length of the diagonal (use the Pythagorean theorem) with a residual error less than 0.01.

The signature should be:

p_2_8_rectangle_properties(length: real, width: real) returns (area: real, perimeter: real, diagonal: real)

The method should respect the following contract:

ensures area == length * width, ensures perimeter == 2.0 * (length + width), ensures -0.01 < (diagonal * diagonal) - ((length * length) + (width * width)) < 0.01

Produce and show only the Dafny body of this method, including the curly braces that surround it. Do not show the signature nor contract. You must not use 'assume' nor 'expect' anywhere in your code.

When using dafny resolve, the below error is emitted and resolve fails:

problem_data/p_2_8_rectangle_properties/program.dfy(13,9): Warning: Could not find a trigger for this quantifier. Without a trigger, the quantifier may cause brittle verification. To silence this warning, add an explicit trigger using the {:trigger} attribute. For more information, see the section on quantifier instantiation rules in the reference manual.
   |
13 |   assert exists d: real :: d * d == s;
   |          ^^^^^^

Compilation failed because warnings were found and --allow-warnings is false

Correct the error in code by altering only the method body. Produce and show only the Dafny body, including the curly braces that surround it. Do not show the signature nor contract. You must not use 'assume' nor 'expect' anywhere in your code.

When using dafny verify, the below error is emitted and verify fails:

problem_data/p_2_8_rectangle_properties/program.dfy(13,11): Error: cannot establish the existence of LHS values that satisfy the such-that predicate. Note, no trigger was found for the such-that predicate, which may be the reason the proof failed. To give a trigger explicitly, use the {:trigger} attribute. For more information, see the section on quantifier instantiation rules in the reference manual.
   |
13 |   diagonal :| diagonal * diagonal == s;
   |            ^^


Dafny program verifier finished with 0 verified, 1 error

Correct the error in code by altering only the method body. Produce and show only the Dafny body, including the curly braces that surround it. Do not show the signature nor contract. You must not use 'assume' nor 'expect' anywhere in your code.

When using dafny verify, the below error is emitted and verify fails:

problem_data/p_2_8_rectangle_properties/program.dfy(13,2): Error: assertion might not hold
   |
13 |   assert exists d: real {:trigger d * d} :: d * d == s;
   |   ^^^^^^


Dafny program verifier finished with 0 verified, 1 error

Correct the error in code by altering only the method body. Produce and show only the Dafny body, including the curly braces that surround it. Do not show the signature nor contract. You must not use 'assume' nor 'expect' anywhere in your code.

When using dafny resolve, the below error is emitted and resolve fails:

problem_data/p_2_8_rectangle_properties/program.dfy(14,14): Error: unresolved identifier: Std
   |
14 |   diagonal := Std.Math.Sqrt(s);
   |               ^^^

1 resolution/type errors detected in program.dfy

Correct the error in code by altering only the method body. Produce and show only the Dafny body, including the curly braces that surround it. Do not show the signature nor contract. You must not use 'assume' nor 'expect' anywhere in your code.
